tagline: Enhance metric views with dynamic parameters for flexible querying.
what_it_does: Metric views can now include parameters that allow users to pass values at query time, enabling a single metric view definition to serve multiple query variants. Parameters can be used in various SQL expressions, including filters and joins, and can have default values for convenience. This reduces the need for multiple metric view definitions, streamlining maintenance and enhancing usability.
why_it_matters: This update significantly improves productivity by reducing duplication in metric view definitions, allowing data teams to manage fewer artifacts while still providing flexibility in querying. It also enhances the ability to adapt metrics to varying business scenarios without additional overhead, ultimately leading to better decision-making and faster insights.
plain_summary: Metric views can now include parameters that allow users to pass values at query time, enabling a single metric view definition to serve multiple query variants. Parameters can be used in various SQL expressions, including filters and joins, and can have…

capabilities.title: Dynamic Parameterization
capabilities.desc: Define parameters in metric views to customize queries at runtime.

capabilities.title: Default Values
capabilities.desc: Set default values for parameters, simplifying queries for users who do not specify them.

capabilities.title: Low-Code Editor Support
capabilities.desc: Easily add parameters using the Catalog Explorer low-code editor.

capabilities.title: Flexible Querying
capabilities.desc: Query metric views using parameters by name or position, enhancing usability.

prerequisites: Compute resource must meet minimum version requirements for metric views.
use_cases: Dynamic reporting based on user-selected date ranges.
Customizing metrics for different discount rates in sales analysis.
Adapting metrics for various geographical regions without creating separate views.
limitations: Materialization is not supported for metric views that define parameters.

steps.title: Add Parameter
steps.desc: In the low-code editor, click 'Add parameter' and enter the parameter details.

steps.title: Define Default Value
steps.desc: Specify a default value and data type for the parameter.

steps.title: Save Parameter
steps.desc: Click 'Save' to finalize the parameter definition.

steps.title: Query with Parameters
steps.desc: Call the metric view as a table-valued function, passing parameters as needed.

steps.title: View Parameter Definitions
steps.desc: Check the YAML definition to see the parameters defined for the metric view.

key_takeaway: The introduction of parameters in metric views allows for more efficient and flexible data querying, reducing maintenance overhead and enhancing user experience.
